sovereign_credit_rating.value: S&P: NR, Moody's: NR, Fitch: NR

resource_self_sufficiency_index.methodology: Average of min(production/consumption, 1.0) for energy, food, water, minerals

political_risk_premium_bps.methodology: Sovereign rating implied spread + adjustments for instability, conflict, sanctions, autocracy

market_accessibility_score.methodology: Average of capital_account_openness, stock_market_cap/200, financial_dev_index, ease_of_business/100

narrative.executive_summary: Iran faces an existential crisis following US-Israeli Operation Epic Fury on Feb 28, which struck 1,000+ targets across 24 provinces and killed Supreme Leader Khamenei. Iranian retaliation targeted 27 US bases and Gulf Arab states. The Strait of Hormuz experienced a 70% traffic collapse. With no clear succession mechanism, comprehensive sanctions, 42.4% inflation, and active military conflict, Iran is effectively uninvestable. Political risk premium stands at 2,200 bps.
key_changes_this_week: US-Israeli joint strikes on Feb 28 killed Supreme Leader Khamenei and struck 1,000+ targets across Iran (transformative)
Iran retaliated with missiles/drones on 27 US bases and Gulf states including UAE, Qatar, Kuwait, Bahrain, Oman
Strait of Hormuz traffic collapsed 70%; IRGC attacked 3 commercial ships and broadcast closure warnings
40 days of mourning declared; Assembly of Experts must select new Supreme Leader amid active warfare
Oil surged 13% to $82/barrel with forecasts of $100/barrel if conflict persists
narrative.outlook: Iran faces the most severe compound crisis in its modern history: regime decapitation, active military conflict with the world's foremost military power, infrastructure devastation, and economic collapse trajectory. The succession process under the Assembly of Experts has no precedent under wartime conditions. The 90-day constitutional timeline for selecting a new Supreme Leader will be tested against ongoing military operations and potential internal power struggles.
narrative.investor_implications: Iran is uninvestable for all practical purposes. Downstream exposure is the key concern: monitor any portfolio holdings with Iran trade links, Iranian diaspora banking flows, or sanctions compliance obligations. The primary transmission channel for global investors is through energy prices and Hormuz-dependent supply chains.
narrative.data_quality_note: Iran data quality was already constrained pre-conflict due to sanctions and limited reporting. Current situation renders most economic projections highly speculative. No rated sovereign credit. Inflation and exchange rate data likely understated given wartime conditions.
